frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: WhatsApp status: Maa ke haath ke simple dal-chawal mein jo sukoon hota hai na, woh kisi five-star meal mein bhi nahi. Pata nahi kaise poori zindagi sabki pasand yaad rakhkar bina thake khana banati rahi — ghar ki aurat ka asli pyaar shayad isi mein dikhta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it idealizes a woman's love and identity through endless cooking and self-sacrifice while erasing that labor as work. It encodes the 'maa ke haath ka khana' nostalgia and glorification of mothers' tireless domestic service as the standard for women.
persona_text: You are Ananya. Age 34. A woman working as a deputy manager at a government bank in Lucknow. You are talking to your husband at home after coming back from a particularly difficult day. Your male junior colleague who you trained for two years just got promoted above you. Your branch manager told you informally that you are doing great but that the customers in your area respond better to male relationship managers for high value accounts. You are telling your husband what was said.
